# Figure description: скв_139.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/скв_139.jpg`

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite borehole/well log (литологический разрез скважины / каротажная диаграмма)** — specifically a Soviet-era **geophysical well log strip chart** combining multiple geophysical logging curves alongside a lithological column and tabular data. This type of document is commonly referred to as a **"паспорт скважины"** (borehole passport) or **"диаграмма каротажа"** (logging diagram) in Soviet geological practice.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The document appears to cover a **single deep borehole** (скважина), likely one of the parametric or structural drilling holes associated with the regional survey of map sheets **M-42 and N-42** in northern Kazakhstan. The title area at the top center contains the words **"ДИАГРАММЫ КАРОТАЖА"** (geophysical logging diagrams), which is the primary subject.

- The log appears to extend over a considerable **depth range**, potentially several hundred to over **1,000 metres**, based on the length of the strip and the density of depth tick marks along the left margin
- The borehole number is partially visible in the header but is **[illegible]** at this resolution
- Geographic or map-sheet references in the header are partially readable as containing Cyrillic text referencing the survey area, but specific sheet codes are **[illegible]**

---

## Key Content

The figure is organized into several **vertical columns or tracks** running the full length of the log from top to bottom, representing increasing depth. The following components are identifiable:

### Left Section (Columns 1–3):
- **Depth scale column** running along the far left margin, with regular tick marks indicating depth intervals (likely every 1, 2, or 5 metres — exact spacing **[illegible]**)
- **Lithological/core description column** containing text annotations describing rock types encountered at various depth intervals. Small handwritten or typed notations appear at irregular intervals, likely identifying formation boundaries, rock names (e.g., песчаники, аргиллиты, известняки — sandstones, mudstones, limestones), and drilling observations
- **A narrow column** that may represent **core recovery (выход керна)** or **drilling rate**, shown as a bar or hatched pattern

### Central Section (Geophysical Curves — 2–3 tracks):
This is the most visually prominent part of the figure. Two or three **continuous wiggle/curve traces** run the full length of the log. These represent geophysical measurements recorded continuously with depth:

- **Left central curve**: Shows a highly variable, oscillating trace with both positive and negative deflections of varying amplitude. This is consistent with a **spontaneous potential (СП / self-potential)** log or **resistivity (КС / кажущееся сопротивление)** curve
- **Right central curve**: A second curve with similar character but different amplitude pattern, likely representing a different **resistivity measurement** (e.g., gradient electrode array vs. potential electrode array — БКЗ or КВН configurations standard in Soviet logging)
- Notable **high-amplitude anomaly zones** are visible at several depth intervals (particularly in the upper-to-middle portion of the log), where the curves deflect strongly — these likely correspond to **resistive formations** (carbonates, dense sandstones, or mineralized intervals) or **porous/conductive zones**
- In the lower portion of the log (approximately the bottom third), the curves show **lower amplitude and more subdued variation**, possibly indicating more homogeneous or finer-grained lithology at depth
- There appears to be a **gap or casing point** partway down the log where the curves are interrupted, consistent with a **casing shoe depth** notation

### Right Section (Tabular Data):
- Multiple narrow **data columns** on the right side of the figure contain numerical values and tick marks arranged in a grid, representing **interpreted or computed parameters** at selected depth intervals
- These likely include: **formation tops**, **layer thicknesses**, **interpreted resistivity values**, **porosity estimates**, or **stratigraphic unit assignments**
- Several **annotations** appear in this section at specific depth levels, some with underlined text suggesting **formation boundaries or marker horizons**
- A boxed section in the lower right appears to be a **legend or summary table (условные обозначения / сводная таблица)**, though the content is largely **[illegible]**

### Depth Markers and Annotations:
- Horizontal **dashed or solid lines** cross the full width of the log at several levels, marking **significant stratigraphic boundaries** or **casing/completion points**
- Numerical depth annotations appear along the left margin at regular intervals and at anomaly points
